name: Guadix Vega and Cerro del Humilladero Trail
name_original: Vega de Guadix y Cerro del Humilladero – trail running circular desde Guadix
description: Jog the moderate Guadix Vega and Cerro del Humilladero Trail, a 5.4-mile route through unique Badlands geology and scenic views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Guadix offer a diverse landscape characterized by its unique badlands topography, dramatic gullies, and the distinctive cave-houses. The region sits at the foothills of the Sierra Nevada, providing varied terrain from accessible paths to more challenging trails with elevation changes. Runners can explore semi-arid landscapes, river plains, and areas featuring impressive geological formations. This environment provides a unique setting for running, blending natural beauty with cultural landmarks. Best jogging routes around Guadix The most popular jogging route is Guadix Vega and Cerro del Humilladero Trail, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Vega de Guadix and gentle elevation changes. Another top favourite among local runners is Cave Quarter, Guadix – Mirador de la Magdalena loop from Guadix, a difficult 12.3 miles (19.8 km) path. This route explores the unique cave-house district and offers panoramic views from Mirador de la Magdalena. Local runners also love the Mirador de la Magdalena loop from Guadix, a 11.8 miles (19.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Jogging around Guadix is defined by its badlands, unique cave-house neighborhoods, and views towards the Sierra Nevada foothills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more demanding trails. The routes in Guadix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 200 visitors have used komoot to explore Guadix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Guadix?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Guadix listed on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What makes jogging in Guadix a unique experience?
A: Jogging in Guadix is unique due to its distinctive landscape, characterized by dramatic badlands, eroded gullies, and the famous cave-houses. You can run through semi-arid, almost lunar landscapes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert&lt;/a&gt; or explore the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6088243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave Quarter&lt;/a&gt;, all while enjoying views towards the Sierra Nevada foothills.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Guadix?
A: Yes, Guadix offers routes suitable for beginners. While many trails are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy route available. For a moderate option with gentle elevation changes, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985176986/guadix-vega-and-cerro-del-humilladero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadix Vega and Cerro del Humilladero Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 8.7 km long.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running options in Guadix?
A: Absolutely. Guadix provides plenty of challenging trail running options, with 12 routes classified as difficult. These often feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14597287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Magdalena loop from Guadix&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 19 km with substantial climbing.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Guadix?
A: Guadix's running routes offer stunning natural features and unique landmarks. You can encounter the vibrant red hues of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive geological formations of the Marchal Gullies, or the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6399178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchal Cave Houses&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also provide panoramic views from various viewpoints, showcasing the vastness of the Olla de Guadix and Geopark.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Guadix?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Guadix are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33873586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave Quarter, Guadix loop from Guadix&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 15.9 km circular route that explores the unique cave-house district.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Guadix?
A: The running routes in Guadix are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. Many runners praise the unique landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with cultural sights like the cave-houses, and the variety of terrain available.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the famous cave-house district?
A: Yes, you can definitely incorporate the famous cave-house district into your run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave Quarter, Guadix – Mirador de la Magdalena loop from Guadix&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 19.8 km route that takes you through this intriguing area, offering a blend of exercise and cultural sightseeing.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Guadix?
A: Guadix offers good conditions for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. While summer can be hot, early mornings or late evenings are still viable. Winter running is also possible, especially on lower-altitude routes, though the Sierra Nevada foothills may experience colder conditions.

Q: Can I find routes with good viewpoints in Guadix?
A: Yes, Guadix is known for its spectacular viewpoints. The 'End of the World' Viewpoints (Mirador del Fin del Mundo) offer expansive panoramas of the Olla de Guadix and Geopark. You can experience great views on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Padre Poveda Viewpoint of Guadix loop from Guadix&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8 km run.
tips: The path of the Cerro del Humilladero, marked out by the Guadix Town Hall, runs along traditional agricultural paths that bring us closer to the gullies and reveal the landscapes of the Vega de Guadix. Along the path you will also find some information panels that support the interpretation of the route.
Construction began in 1492 on the site of the former Moorish mosque. However, it took centuries to complete.

Guadix is located in the province of Granada on the banks of the Río Guadix.
Guadix is known for its cave districts made of soft tufa rock.
There were about 2000 dwelling caves.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Guadix Vega and Cerro del Humilladero Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Guadix. You can typically find parking within the town of Guadix, especially near the starting points of local trails. There isn't a specific dedicated trailhead parking lot mentioned, so look for public parking options in Guadix.

faqs.question: Is the Guadix Vega and Cerro del Humilladero Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, most trails in this region are dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Remember to pack out any waste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the region's climate and the trail's moderate difficulty, spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) are generally the best times. The weather is milder, making for a more comfortable jogging experience. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be chilly.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Guadix Vega and Cerro del Humilladero Trail. It is a public trail open for recreational use.

faqs.question: Are there any special regulations or fees for the Geoparque de Granada where this trail is located?
faqs.answer: The Guadix Vega and Cerro del Humilladero Trail is located within the Geoparque de Granada. While there are no specific entrance fees for the geopark itself, visitors are encouraged to respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and follow Leave No Trace principles to preserve its unique geological formations and ecosystems.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of mostly paved and easily accessible paths, suitable for jogging. It also incorporates traditional agricultural paths. You'll experience the distinctive 'Badlands' geology of the Guadix region, characterized by solid clay formations and impressive gullies, or 'cárcavas'.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Joggers can enjoy picturesque views of the Vega de Guadix landscapes and its characteristic gullies. The route passes by the Cerro del Humilladero Trail. While not directly on the trail, the unique cave houses of Guadix are a significant part of the regional scenery, and the Cave Quarter, Guadix is nearby. You might also catch glimpses of the Guadix Cathedral (Cathedral of the Incarnation) from certain viewpoints. Information panels are present to help interpret the surroundings.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers a significant portion of the 'Vega de Guadix y Cerro del Humilladero' official trail (74%) and also passes through smaller sections of other routes such as 'El Camino Espiritual del Sur' (12%) and 'Camino Mozárabe. Almería-Granada' (11%).

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Guadix Vega and Cerro del Humilladero Trail is rated as 'moderate' for jogging, requiring 'good fitness'. While some shorter versions might be considered easier, this 8.7 km route with its elevation changes is best suited for those with some jogging experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers fantastic views of the Rambla de Zaraguhit, Sierra Nevada, and the Altiplano de Guadix, especially from the area around the Cerro del Humilladero. The unique 'Badlands' geology with its impressive gullies also serves as a significant natural landmark throughout the route.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Wear comfortable jogging attire suitable for the weather conditions, and sturdy running shoes with good grip, as parts of the trail include agricultural paths. Bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months, and sun protection like a hat and sunscreen. A small backpack for essentials is also recommended.
